methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The total allowance granted to all vaults for a specific ERC20 token must never exceed the Router's actual balance of that token.
invariants.attack_scenario: An attacker exploits a logic flaw (e.g., reentrancy or fake deposits) to increase a vault's allowance without transferring actual tokens. They can then drain tokens deposited by other users, rendering the protocol insolvent.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_solvency(address token) public view { uint256 total = 0; for(uint i=0; i<vaults.length; i++) { total += vaultAllowance(vaults[i], token); } assert(total <= IERC20(token).balanceOf(address(this))); }

invariants.description: When transferring allowance between vaults within the same router, the total allowance across all vaults must remain strictly constant.
invariants.attack_scenario: A vulnerability in allowance transfer allows a vault to duplicate allowance (e.g., by transferring to itself or exploiting underflows), leading to unauthorized token extraction.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_allowance_conservation(address newVault, address token, uint256 amount) public { uint pre = vaultAllowance(msg.sender, token) + vaultAllowance(newVault, token); transferAllowance(address(this), newVault, token, amount, ""); uint post = vaultAllowance(msg.sender, token) + vaultAllowance(newVault, token); assert(pre == post); }

invariants.description: The allowance credited to a vault upon deposit must exactly equal the actual increase in the Router's token balance, ensuring solvency even with fee-on-transfer tokens.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the Router credits the nominal deposit amount instead of the actual received amount for a fee-on-transfer token, the total allowance will exceed the Router's balance, allowing the vault to steal other users' funds.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_deposit_allowance(address vault, address token, uint256 amount) public { uint256 preBal = IERC20(token).balanceOf(address(this)); uint256 preAllow = vaultAllowance(vault, token); _deposit(payable(vault), token, amount, ""); uint256 postBal = IERC20(token).balanceOf(address(this)); uint256 postAllow = vaultAllowance(vault, token); assert(postAllow - preAllow == postBal - preBal); }

invariants.description: The Router contract should not retain any ETH balance from deposits or transfers; all handled ETH must be immediately forwarded to the designated vault or recipient.
invariants.attack_scenario: If ETH is not properly forwarded due to a logic error or gas limit issue, it becomes trapped in the Router contract, leading to a loss of user funds.

invariants.description: The aggregator must not leak value during swapIn; the entire ETH balance received from the DEX swap must be deposited into the router.
invariants.attack_scenario: The aggregator fails to send the full swapped amount to the router, leaving funds trapped in the aggregator and shortchanging the user's cross-chain swap.
